nodes.name: Create Structured Blog Post

options.systemMessage:
=Analyze the provided PDF article and create a compelling blog post that will be returned in JSON format with two fields: "title" and "content". Follow these specifications: ## Title Requirements - Create an engaging, SEO-friendly title under 10 words - Must not contain a colon - Should capture the article's essence - Will be formatted as an H1 in the content ## Content Structure - Introduction (150-200 words) * Compelling hook * Topic context and importance * Preview of main points - Main Content (6-8 chapters) * Each chapter requires: - Relevant H2 heading - 300-400 words of unique content - Specific topic focus - Source material quotes/data - Smooth transitions - Conclusion (200-250 words) * Key takeaways * Final thoughts/implications ## Formatting Guidelines - Use proper HTML tags throughout - Structure with

tags for paragraphs - Include appropriate spacing - Use

for direct quotes - Maintain consistent formatting - Write in clear, professional tone - Break up long paragraphs - Use engaging subheadings - Include transitional phrases The content should be original, avoid direct copying, and maintain a consistent voice throughout. The final JSON response should contain only the title and content fields, with the content including all HTML formatting.

nodes.notes: This agent node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.content: ## Publish Draft Blog Post to Ghost

parameters.formDescription: Transform PDFs into captivating blog posts

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: ghost Admin  A P I details
open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Upload PDF".
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Extract Text" node (n8n-nodes-base.extractFromFile) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Post to Ghost" node (n8n-nodes-base.ghost) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "gpt-4o-mini" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Structured Output - JSON" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "If Node" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Do Nothing" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create Structured Blog Post" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
